sections.title: Is New York City Safe for Solo Female Travelers?
sections.summary: A calm, practical overview before we dive into NYC-specific scenarios.
bullets: New York is one of the safer big cities in the U.S., and the areas most visitors spend time in — Midtown, the Upper West Side, Brooklyn Heights, DUMBO — are busy and well-lit most of the day. As a solo woman you get a lot of freedom here; the main things to think about are late nights and the subway.

This guide sticks to actual situations: crowded streets, unwanted attention, the subway after dark, and which neighborhoods are easy to base yourself in. No scare stories — just what to do when each comes up.
Whether you're in a museum, walking across Manhattan, or on the train home after dinner, the point is the same: stay aware, and know your move if something feels off.
